description: Phase 3 — UX Polish, Push Notifications, and Production Hardening. Make SoarCast feel like a polished, production-ready app that pilots actually want to use daily. Focus on UX refinement, smart notifications, performance, and reliability.

context.stack: Next.js 16 + Turso (Drizzle ORM) + shadcn/ui + Leaflet + Tailwind v4
context.existingFeatures: Interactive windgrams with lapse rate, wind barbs, thermal indicators, interactivity. 55 WA launch sites imported. Atmospheric profile API. Flyability scoring and summaries. Dashboard with thumbnails.
context.designValues: Beautiful yet minimalistic. Friendly UIs. Strong UX focus. Clean code, symmetry. Ship things useful to people.

userStories.title: Make Browse & Site Pages Public
userStories.description: Site browse page and individual site detail pages (with windgrams) must be accessible without authentication. Only user-specific actions (favoriting, custom sites, settings) require sign-in.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /sites/browse is accessible without sign-in
GET /sites/[slug] is accessible without sign-in (windgram visible)
GET /api/weather/profile is accessible without sign-in
GET /api/sites (read-only list) is accessible without sign-in
GET /api/sites/[slug] is accessible without sign-in
Favorite button shows sign-in prompt for unauthenticated users instead of hiding
Dashboard, My Sites, Settings still require authentication
Custom site creation/editing still requires authentication
npm run build succeeds
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: The middleware in src/middleware.ts currently blocks ALL /sites/* routes. Update protectedRoutes to only protect mutation endpoints and user-specific pages. The API routes /api/sites and /api/weather/profile should be public for read access.

userStories.title: Smart Push Notifications with Windgram Data
userStories.description: Upgrade the existing push notification system to use atmospheric profile data for smarter, more accurate fly/no-fly alerts. Currently notifications just use the simple scoring — integrate windgram-quality analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Notification check (/api/notifications/check) uses atmospheric profile data in addition to simple scoring
Notifications include: flyability rating, best window, key concerns, thermal strength estimate
Notification message is plain-language: 'Tiger Mountain looks Epic tomorrow! Best thermals 11AM-2PM, light SW winds. Cloud base ~5000ft.'
Users can configure notification preferences per site: minimum rating threshold, time of day to receive
Morning digest option: one notification at user's preferred time summarizing all favorited sites
npm run build succeeds
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: The existing notification system checks twice daily at 6AM and 6PM UTC. Keep that schedule but make the messages much richer using the atmospheric profile analysis.

userStories.title: Site Search & Discovery Improvements
userStories.description: Make it easy to find and discover launch sites. Current browse page has basic search — make it great.
acceptanceCriteria: Full-text search across site name, region, country
Filter by: flyability today (show only sites rated Good+), orientation, site type, country/region
Sort by: distance from user (geolocation), flyability score today, name, recently added
Map clusters when zoomed out (don't show 55 individual markers at state level)
URL reflects current filters (shareable links: /sites/browse?region=washington&minScore=good)
Recent searches / recently viewed sites for quick access
npm run build succeeds
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: For distance sorting, use the browser Geolocation API with a fallback. Leaflet has marker clustering plugins (leaflet.markercluster). Keep the map responsive and fast.

userStories.title: Onboarding Flow for New Users
userStories.description: First-time users should immediately understand SoarCast and get value without signing in. Guide them to their first useful forecast.
acceptanceCriteria: Landing page clearly explains what SoarCast does in 5 seconds (not a generic tech landing page)
Immediate value without sign-in: browse sites, view windgrams, check forecasts
Gentle prompt to sign in when they try to favorite a site or enable notifications
After sign-in: guided flow to favorite their home site(s) or pick from nearby sites (geolocation)
Onboarding is skippable — experienced users can dismiss and go straight to dashboard
Mobile-first: the onboarding works great on phones (pilots often on mobile)
npm run build succeeds
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Most paraglider pilots will discover SoarCast on their phone, shared by another pilot. First impression matters. Show a beautiful windgram for a well-known site as the hero, then let them explore.

userStories.title: Performance & Loading Optimization
userStories.description: Make everything fast. Atmospheric data fetches can be slow — optimize with smart caching, prefetching, and progressive loading.
acceptanceCriteria: Site detail page loads site info instantly, then progressively loads windgram data
Dashboard prefetches today's atmospheric data for favorited sites in the background
API responses have appropriate Cache-Control headers (profile data: 1 hour, site info: 24 hours)
Windgram chart renders progressively: layout first, then lapse rate, then wind barbs, then indicators
Service worker caches site data and recent profiles for offline-capable viewing
Lighthouse performance score >= 80 on mobile for the dashboard page
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: The existing service worker (public/sw.js) handles basic caching. Extend it to cache atmospheric profile responses. Use stale-while-revalidate for profile data. Consider using React Server Components more effectively to reduce client JS.

userStories.title: Error Handling & Reliability
userStories.description: Handle failures gracefully. Open-Meteo could be down, Turso could timeout, the user could be offline. The app should degrade gracefully, not crash.
acceptanceCriteria: Open-Meteo API failure: show cached data with 'Last updated X hours ago' badge, or a friendly error message if no cache
Turso timeout: retry with backoff, show appropriate error state
Offline: show cached data, indicate offline status, queue favorites/settings changes for sync
Windgram component handles missing/incomplete atmospheric data gracefully (empty pressure levels, null values)
Global error boundary catches unexpected crashes with a useful error page (not a white screen)
All error states have a retry button
Sentry or similar error tracking configured (use NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN env var, optional)
npm run build succeeds
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: The existing error.tsx and global-error.tsx are minimal. Make them actually useful. For offline support, rely on the service worker cache and add a visible indicator. Don't add Sentry as a hard dependency — make it optional via env var.

userStories.title: Responsive Design Audit & Mobile Polish
userStories.description: Go through every page on mobile (360px-428px) and tablet (768px) and fix any layout issues. SoarCast must feel native-app quality on mobile since most pilots use it on their phones.
acceptanceCriteria: All pages render correctly at 360px, 390px, 428px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px
Windgram chart is touch-friendly: pinch to zoom, swipe between days, tap for details
Navigation works smoothly on mobile (sidebar collapses to bottom nav or hamburger)
No horizontal scroll on any page at any breakpoint
Font sizes readable on mobile without zooming
Buttons and tap targets are at least 44px (Apple HIG minimum)
PWA: app icon, splash screen, fullscreen mode all work correctly
Playwright tests for key flows at mobile viewport (390px width)
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use Playwright's setViewportSize for mobile testing. The windgram chart needs special attention on mobile — consider a simplified view that shows key info without requiring precise hover targeting.
